failure_surface: Repeated syntax errors during code execution attempts prevented any workbook manipulation from occurring
root_cause: Agent repeatedly submitted tool invocations with malformed wrapper format causing parser-level syntax errors before code execution, indicating failure to validate tool invocation structure
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Validate tool invocation format before submission; when receiving parser-level syntax errors pointing to invocation wrapper characters, check the tool call structure rather than modifying only the inner code logic
evidence: Multiple execute_code/write_file attempts all produced identical SyntaxError at line 1 showing raw wrapper characters instead of valid Python
Error consistently appeared at 'Cell In[1], line 1' pointing to tool wrapper artifact rather than logical code issues
Agent attempted 4+ times without recognizing the wrapper format was the problem source
The actual Python logic for Excel formula manipulation would have been valid if executed properly

memory_items.title: Tool Invocation Format Validation
memory_items.lesson: Parser-level syntax errors pointing to wrapper artifacts indicate malformed tool calls, not code logic errors
memory_items.generalization: When execution fails immediately at line 1 with non-Python characters visible, inspect the invocation format before modifying code logic

memory_items.title: Iterative Debugging on Repeated Failures
memory_items.lesson: Identical error messages across multiple attempts suggest a persistent structural issue rather than incremental bugs
memory_items.generalization: Upon 2+ consecutive identical failures, pause code modification and audit the surrounding framework/tool usage pattern

memory_items.title: Execution Environment Compatibility Check
memory_items.lesson: Tool wrappers must match the execution environment's expected syntax before code validation occurs
memory_items.generalization: Ensure tool invocation format complies with the target execution system before assuming code correctness

edits.content:
## Execution Hygiene

### Tool Invocation Validity
- Validate tool call format before submission; parser errors at line 1 often indicate wrapper issues, not code problems
- When identical syntax errors occur consecutively, audit the invocation structure before modifying logic
- Ensure execution environment compatibility for tool wrappers before testing code functionality

### Failure Pattern Recognition
- Distinguish between semantic errors (logic/runtime) and syntactic errors (format/wrapper)
- Parser-level failures require format fixes; runtime failures require code fixes
- Log error patterns across iterations to avoid repeating the same structural mistakes
